instance_reference.note: Per-chip-hour figures derived from the whole-instance hourly price divided by 16 Trainium2 chips.

price_note: AWS does NOT publish an official on-demand list price for trn2.48xlarge on its product/pricing pages (checked 2026-07-17); on_demand is the figure replicated by two independent secondary sources (~$23.97-24/hr per instance, Feb 2026) = $1.50/chip-hr. CONFLICT: one Jun-2026 secondary source reports ~$38.47/hr (= $2.40/chip-hr); treat on_demand as uncertain by +/-60%. reserved_1y is null because no 1-year reserved rate is publicly listed; ac.pricing falls back to on_demand when the requested tier is null. Spot is a structured price-tracker snapshot (~$8.60-8.72/hr per instance in us-east-2).
energy_note: AWS does not publish Trainium2 TDP; 650W is a SECONDARY-ESTIMATE (reporting aligned with SemiAnalysis modeling), tier T3. utilization, PUE (1.15, assumption) and $0.10/kWh are ASSUMPTIONS, not measurements.

prices.derivation: trn2.48xlarge ~$23.97-24/hr / 16 chips, two independent secondary sources (Feb 2026). Conflicting report: ~$38.47/hr (Jun 2026, stackpulsar) = $2.40/chip-hr; lower replicated figure chosen, uncertainty flagged.
No publicly listed 1-year reserved rate for trn2.48xlarge found as of 2026-07-17; null = caller falls back to on_demand.
trn2.48xlarge spot $8.60-8.72/hr / 16 chips (us-east-2 structured price trackers, snapshot; spot is dynamic)

tdp.note: AWS does not publish Trainium2 TDP. 650W from secondary reporting (spec table aligned with SemiAnalysis modeling); treat as approximate.

pue.note: ASSUMPTION. AWS does not publish fleet-average PUE.
